creator: jwb@freebsd.org
text:
I'd like to use x11-themes/sddm-freebsd-black-theme as the default in sysutils/desktop-installer, but it has one fatal flaw:

If the session menu becomes too large, as in after installing gnome, some sessions at the bottom cannot be reached.  I don't know if SDDM can scroll the menu in other themes, but placement of the menu at the top of the screen in the default theme makes it a non-issue.

This is mainly an issue for lower resolutions displays, such as VirtualBox VMs, but I think it would affect larger screens if KDE and Gnome are both installed, as they both add multiple session options.
